key_points: Trump cancelled a planned AI safety executive order hours before signing, citing concern over US lead vs China
Tech billionaires Musk, Zuckerberg, and Sacks lobbied Trump directly to kill the order
The order would have created a voluntary government review process for new AI models — with no legal enforcement
Discussions began after Anthropic's Claude Mythos model raised serious cybersecurity concerns
Prospects for any meaningful AI regulation under the Trump administration are now extremely unlikely
reference_summary: Trump abruptly cancelled a planned AI safety executive order after Elon Musk, Mark Zuckerberg, and David Sacks lobbied him directly, arguing it would cede ground to China. The scrapped order would have created a non-binding voluntary review for AI models, and the episode signals the administration has no appetite for AI regulation despite growing security concerns.
